display.purpose: Silkscreen, full caps — the landing wordmark (BENDOBUNDLES) and nowhere else.

title.purpose: Pixelify Sans — dialog headings, the claim question and celebration.

body.purpose: Chivo — every paragraph and sentence. Prose is always human.

label.purpose: Pixelify Sans at 13px — chips, badges, helper text.

mono.purpose: Gift keys, link tokens, appids. Never voice copy.

shadows.name: ceremony
shadows.purpose: Dialogs and gift/claim moments only — paired with a Pixel bezel ring.

motion.name: landing-rise
motion.purpose: The landing's single quiet entrance; prefers-reduced-motion disables it.

components.name: Claim Button
components.description: The primary action — Button Burgundy, the DMG A/B button color. Canonical instance: the claim button.
components.css: .ds-btn-give { background: oklch(46% 0.155 355); color: oklch(97% 0.012 350); padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: none; border-radius: 0.25rem; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-give:hover { background: oklch(52% 0.16 355); } .ds-btn-give:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(35% 0.06 120); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-give:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Neutral Button
components.description: Retry / connect / cancel-type actions in the room's own green.
components.css: .ds-btn-neutral { background: oklch(73% 0.09 113); color: oklch(30% 0.06 120); padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 0.875rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.25rem; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-neutral:hover { background: oklch(69% 0.09 114); } .ds-btn-neutral:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(35% 0.06 120); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Shelf Chip
components.description: Informational tag — platform, bundle, claim state. The gifted badge wears Button Burgundy.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; background: oklch(80% 0.09 112); color: oklch(36% 0.06 118); padding: 2px 8px; font-size: 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; } .ds-chip-gifted { background: oklch(46% 0.155 355); color: oklch(97% 0.012 350); }

components.name: Game Card
components.description: The signature container: edge-bleed art (or title-hash block) over a matte floor frame.
components.html:
a short hike

humble choice may 2020

steam
components.css: .ds-game-card { width: 260px; background: oklch(91% 0.07 110); border-radius: 0.5rem; overflow: hidden; } .ds-game-art { aspect-ratio: 16 / 9; background: oklch(43.2% 0.232 292.759); } .ds-game-body { padding: 16px; } .ds-game-title { margin: 0; color: oklch(30% 0.06 120); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.25; } .ds-game-bundle { margin: 4px 0 8px; color: oklch(43% 0.06 116); font-size: 0.75rem; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; } .ds-chip-mini { display: inline-block; background: oklch(80% 0.09 112); color: oklch(36% 0.06 118); padding: 2px 8px; font-size: 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; }

components.name: Gift Dialog
components.description: Where the unwrap happens: floor panel, ceremony shadow, pixel bezel, burgundy celebration.
components.html:
claim a short hike?

this uses 1 of your claims
components.css: .ds-dialog { max-width: 28rem; background: oklch(91% 0.07 110); border-radius: 0.75rem; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 25px 50px -12px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.25), 0 0 0 1px oklch(35% 0.06 120); } .ds-dialog-title { margin: 0; color: oklch(30% 0.06 120); font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; } .ds-dialog-game { color: oklch(42% 0.14 350); } .ds-dialog-sub { margin: 8px 0 0; color: oklch(43% 0.06 116); font-size: 0.875rem; }

components.name: Admin Nav Link
components.description: One quiet row; dust links, ink when active. No sidebar, no chrome.
components.html: cataloglinksops
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; gap: 24px; padding: 12px 24px; border-bottom: 1px solid oklch(66% 0.075 112); background: oklch(87% 0.085 112); } .ds-nav-link { color: oklch(43% 0.06 116); font-size: 0.875rem; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: oklch(36% 0.06 118); } .ds-nav-active { color: oklch(30% 0.06 120); font-weight: 500; }

narrative.northStar: The Attic Arcade — pea soup world
narrative.overview: Fifteen years of Humble Bundle purchases live here the way a game collection lives in an attic — and the most treasured find in that attic is the original 1989 handheld, still working. The whole interface is its screen: light pea-green olive, dark-olive ink, and one saturated accent borrowed from the machine's own A/B buttons — burgundy-magenta, spent only on the act of giving. The original handheld was never backlit; a light theme is the historically honest choice, and the anti-reflex one.
keyCharacteristics: Light, tonal, and monochrome-disciplined: the room is one green, in four-plus shades
One saturated accent (Button Burgundy) reserved for the act of giving/claiming
Games are the color: cover art and the deterministic title-hash palette pop against the olive
Lowercase voice everywhere; ceremony saved for the unwrap
Bezel as motif: chunky dark-olive frames mark ceremony surfaces

rules.name: The Pixel Grammar Rule
rules.body: Pixel type marks game-world things — the logotype, headings, labels, and the burgundy key on the landing. Prose stays human (Chivo). A paragraph in a pixel face is forbidden.

rules.name: The Button Burgundy Rule
rules.body: Burgundy appears only where giving or claiming happens — never as ambient decoration, never above 10% of a screen. Its rarity is what makes the claim button feel like pressing A to accept a gift.

rules.name: The Title-Hash Rule
rules.body: A game without cover art gets its color from the shared deterministic hash (titleColor.ts). The SAME game must render the SAME color on every surface, forever.

rules.name: The Light Text Rule
rules.body: Status text on light surfaces uses the deep end of its hue (green-700, amber-800, red-700) — pale 300/400 tones are invisible on olive and forbidden as inline text.

rules.name: The Lowercase Rule
rules.body: UI copy is lowercase. Caps exist in exactly one place: the Silkscreen logotype renders BENDOBUNDLES full caps via text-transform. Game titles render as their owners spell them.

rules.name: The One Heart Rule
rules.body: The ♡ appears at sincere moments — the landing line, the successful claim — never scattered as decoration. One per screen, maximum, and it must be earned.

rules.name: The Ceremony Rule
rules.body: A drop shadow means a gift moment is happening. Cards, chips, nav, and buttons are flat at every state.

rules.name: The Bezel Rule
rules.body: Chunky dark-olive (Pixel) borders mark the handheld's screen: the landing art frame and dialog rings wear it. A thick border on an ordinary card is costume.

dos: Do reserve Button Burgundy for giving and claiming (≤10% of any screen), pairing every bg-give with text-give-ink.
Do keep every word lowercase and in voice — playful, cozy, sincere.
Do let cover art and the Title-Hash palette carry the saturation; the room stays one green.
Do keep the thin fallback path working: name, color block, and a working claim with no enrichment.
Do use deep status text on light surfaces (green-700 / amber-800 / red-700).
Do trap and return focus in every dialog, and give any motion a prefers-reduced-motion alternative.
donts: Don't import storefront / commerce grammar — no prices, no urgency, no deals language (PRODUCT.md anti-reference).
Don't build SaaS dashboard chrome — no metric-card grids, no sidebar-and-breadcrumb scaffolding (PRODUCT.md anti-reference).
Don't go gamer-RGB edgelord — no neon-on-black, no glow-everything (PRODUCT.md anti-reference).
Don't drift into corporate minimalism — sterile restraint is as off-brand as neon (PRODUCT.md anti-reference).
Don't reintroduce zinc+violet, and don't add new hue families to the room — one green, stepped.
Don't add shadows outside dialogs, side-stripe accent borders, gradient text, or glassmorphism.
Don't scatter hearts. One ♡ per screen, earned.
